title: 'Little House' villain back with warning for new Nellie
body:
In a move that is set to delight fans of the classic series, a beloved actress has made a surprise return to the world of "Little House on the Prairie". The iconic show, which originally aired from 1974 to 1983, is being rebooted by Netflix, and one of its most memorable villains is back to reprise her role. Alison Arngrim, the actress who brought the spoiled and scheming Nellie Oleson to life, has revealed that she will be making a cameo appearance in the new series, and she has some words of wisdom to share with the actress taking on the role of Nellie in the reboot.

The original "Little House on the Prairie" series was based on the books by Laura Ingalls Wilder and followed the lives of the Ingalls family as they navigated the challenges of living on the American frontier in the late 19th century. Nellie Oleson, with her constant scheming and bullying, was a thorn in the side of the Ingalls family, particularly Laura, and her character added a delicious layer of tension and drama to the show. Alison Arngrim's portrayal of Nellie is still widely regarded as one of the most iconic villainous roles in television history, and her return to the series is a major coup for the reboot.

The new "Little House on the Prairie" series is set to bring a fresh perspective to the classic story, with a new cast and a new approach to the material. However, the inclusion of Alison Arngrim in the reboot is a nod to the original series and a way of honoring its legacy. Arngrim's cameo is likely to be a highlight of the new series, and fans of the original show will be eager to see how she brings Nellie back to life. The actress has spoken about the experience of returning to the role, and she has some advice for the new Nellie, urging her to embrace the character's complexity and to have fun with the role.

The reboot of "Little House on the Prairie" is part of a larger trend of classic series being reimagined for modern audiences. With the rise of streaming services, there is a growing demand for content that is both nostalgic and new, and the return of beloved shows like "Little House on the Prairie" is a way of meeting that demand. The inclusion of Alison Arngrim in the reboot is a clever move, as it allows the series to tap into the nostalgia of fans of the original show while also introducing the characters and storylines to a new generation of viewers.

As the new series prepares to launch, fans of "Little House on the Prairie" are eagerly anticipating the return of the Ingalls family and their friends to the small screen. With Alison Arngrim's cameo and the introduction of a new Nellie Oleson, the reboot is shaping up to be a must-watch event. The series is likely to appeal to both old and new fans of the show, and its mix of nostalgia and fresh perspectives is sure to make it a hit with audiences.

As the world of "Little House on the Prairie" comes back to life, it will be interesting to see how the new series is received by fans and critics alike. With its rich characters, engaging storylines, and historical context, the show has the potential to captivate a whole new generation of viewers. And with Alison Arngrim's return to the role of Nellie, the series is off to a strong start. As the reboot launches, fans can look forward to a new take on a classic series, one that combines the best of the old with the excitement of the new.
